hooks.command: input=$(cat); [ "$(printf '%s' "$input" | jq -r '.stop_hook_active // false')" = "true" ] && exit 0; sid=$(printf '%s' "$input" | jq -r '.session_id // empty'); [ -z "$sid" ] && exit 0; m="/tmp/claude-obsidian-stop-$sid"; if [ -f "$m" ]; then age=$(( $(date +%s) - $(stat -f %m "$m") )); [ $age -lt 1200 ] && exit 0; fi; touch "$m"; printf '%s' '{"decision":"block","reason":"Before stopping: if meaningful work has happened since the last save (code changes, decisions, PRs touched, debugging conclusions, follow-ups) — invoke the obsidian-memory skill to append a brief summary to the monthly log. If only trivial activity has occurred (greetings, quick lookups, simple file reads, nothing decided) — just stop without saving. This hook re-prompts every 20 minutes per session, so skipping a trivial moment is fine; real work later will get another chance."}'
